commit.message:
refactor: Standardize tech_stack structure across project

- Update package dependencies by removing gray-matter and js-yaml
- Modify build and validation scripts to handle tech_stack as an object with array support
- Enhance index generation to normalize tech_stack structure
- Update prompt templates to use new tech_stack format with optional service and library arrays

commit.message:
Add custom MDC file parser and update build script

- Create parse-mdc.js to parse .mdc files without external dependencies
- Modify build-index.js to use new parseMdc function
- Update globs in various prompt files to remove quotes
- Improve CONTRIBUTING.md table formatting

Update metadata validation to support optional library field

- Extend Zod schema in validate-structure.js to include optional 'library' field
- Allow metadata to optionally specify a library alongside framework and service
- Maintain existing validation requirements for other metadata fields

Update data/index.json with Next.js and Nuxt 3 coding standards and feature guidelines

- Refactor index.json to include new Next.js and Nuxt 3 coding standards
- Add comprehensive feature guidelines for Next.js 15 and Nuxt 3
- Update file paths and metadata structure
- Remove redundant entries and consolidate prompt information

Expand prompt types in validation script

- Add 'setup' and 'starter' to the list of valid prompt types
- Maintain existing types while extending type support

commit.message:
Update build-index.js to support array-based aiprompt.json files

- Modify getPromptContent() to read individual files specified in metadata
- Update parseFile() to handle array-based JSON files
- Enhance error handling for missing prompt files
- Improve index generation to spread parsed items
- Sort index by name for consistent ordering

Refactor aiprompt.json files to use array format and enhance metadata validation

- Convert single-object JSON files to array format in Laravel, Next.js, and Next.js Shadcn Lucide prompts
- Update validate-structure.js to support array-based metadata validation
- Add 'file' field to metadata schema
- Improve Zod validation for more robust metadata checking

commit.message:
Update package dependencies and remove pnpm-lock.yaml

- Add Zod as a project dependency in package-lock.json
- Remove pnpm-lock.yaml file to standardize package management

Add Zod for robust metadata validation in validate-structure.js

- Integrate Zod for type-safe and comprehensive metadata validation
- Replace manual validation with Zod schema validation
- Add support for optional metadata fields
- Improve error handling and type checking for prompt metadata

readme_html:
AI Prompts & Rules

Instructa AI Prompts is an open-source repository dedicated to collecting and sharing AI prompts, best practices, and curated rules for developers. From project scaffolding to coding standards, our goal is to help you quickly set up and refine your workflow with ready-to-use prompts.

Try it out here or use these prompts directly in your favorite AI coding environment.

Table of Contents

  • 📖 Usage
  • 🤝 Contributing
  • 🛟 Community & Support
  • 🔗 Related Projects
  • ⚖️ License

Usage

To dynamically include prompts in AI-assisted coding tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Zed, Windsurf, and Cline, you can utilize their respective configuration features. This approach ensures that your AI assistant adheres to project-specific coding standards, best practices, and automation workflows.

Need a Guide? Read the blog post: How to use Cursor Rules | X Post

How to Use AI Prompts in Different Tools

AI Tool How to Include Prompts
Cursor Add prompts as project rules inside the .cursor/rules/ directory (e.g., .cursor/rules/cursorrules.mdc). Cursor will automatically detect and apply them. For detailed guidance, refer to the official Cursor rule guide.
GitHub Copilot Create a .github/copilot-instructions.md file in your repository's root directory and add natural language instructions in Markdown format. These instructions will guide Copilot's behavior across your project. More information is available in the GitHub Copilot documentation.
Zed Store prompts in the .zed/ directory within your project. You can configure project-specific settings by creating a .zed/settings.json file, allowing Zed to apply these configurations accordingly. Consult the Zed documentation for further details.
Windsurf Add a .windsurfrules file into the project root. Windsurf Getting Started Guide.
Cline 1. Click Cline extension settings 2. Find "Custom Instructions" field 3. Add your instructions Cline GitHub repository.

By configuring these settings, you can ensure that your AI tools operate in alignment with your project's specific requirements and standards.

🤝 Contributing Prompts

We welcome all contributions! Whether you're adding new prompts, improving existing ones, or fixing typos - every bit helps.

Quick Start:

  1. Create a folder under prompts/<your-prompt-name>
  2. Add metadata in aiprompt.json (see example)
  3. Include .mdc files with YAML front-matter for rules
  4. Submit a Pull Request

For full details, please see our Contribution Guidelines.

Community & Support

  • Discussions: Share ideas, get help, or suggest improvements on our GitHub Discussions.
  • Issues: Report bugs or request new prompt categories through GitHub Issues.

Social

  • X/Twitter: @kregenrek
  • Bluesky: @kevinkern.dev

License

MIT License

This repository is open-source under the MIT license. You’re free to use, modify, and distribute it under those terms. Enjoy building!
